title: Sync fork with upstream advplyr/audiobookshelf
description: This fork is 803 commits behind upstream (advplyr/audiobookshelf). Upstream is at v2.34.0 while this fork is at v2.23.0. We need to sync upstream/master into this fork's master branch. This is blocking meaningful development because any fixes or features we implement may already exist upstream or will conflict heavily. Acceptance criteria: master branch is rebased or merged with upstream/master, all conflicts resolved, tests pass.
close_reason: Fork is already synced with upstream. Master is at 36bc7fc4 which matches upstream/master.

title: Fix test suite: jsonwebtoken buffer-equal-constant-time Node.js compatibility
description:
The test suite fails to run with a TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'prototype') at server/libs/jwa/buffer-equal-constant-time/index.js:37. This is caused by an incompatibility between the vendored jsonwebtoken dependency tree and newer Node.js versions (v25+). In recent Node.js, require('buffer').SlowBuffer is undefined.

## Important Finding
There are TWO copies of buffer-equal-constant-time in this project:
1. node_modules/buffer-equal-constant-time (used by node_modules/jwa, used by ApiKey.js via require('jsonwebtoken'))
2. server/libs/jwa/buffer-equal-constant-time/index.js (used by server/libs/jsonwebtoken, used by server/auth/TokenManager.js)

The vendored copy #2 is what causes the test failure because TokenManager.js loads the vendored jsonwebtoken which loads the vendored jws/jwa which loads the vendored buffer-equal-constant-time.

## Correct Fix
Patch server/libs/jwa/buffer-equal-constant-time/index.js to guard against undefined SlowBuffer. Do NOT use patch-package (node_modules changes won't persist for other developers).

## Acceptance Criteria
npm test runs without this module loading error.
notes: Agent progress: Patched node_modules/buffer-equal-constant-time/index.js via patch-package and added postinstall script to package.json. HOWEVER, tests still fail because server/libs/jwa/buffer-equal-constant-time/index.js (the vendored copy used by server/auth/TokenManager.js) has the SAME SlowBuffer bug. Both files must be fixed. The vendored copy at server/libs/jwa/buffer-equal-constant-time/index.js needs the same SlowBuffer guard. Agent did not commit or close the bead.

title: Implement JWT invalidation on logout
description: Auth.js has a TODO at the logout route: 'TODO: invalidate possible JWTs'. Currently when a user logs out, their JWT token is not invalidated, meaning it remains valid until expiry. This is a security concern. We need to implement a token blacklist or revocation mechanism. Acceptance criteria: After logout, the previously issued JWT cannot be used to access protected routes.

title: Smart Speed Phase 5: Polish, edge cases, and performance
description:
Final polish, edge-case handling, and performance optimization for Smart Speed.

### Scope
- Graceful degradation if AudioWorklet is unsupported (fallback to ScriptProcessorNode or disabled)
- Performance testing and optimization on low-end mobile devices
- Handle very short tracks (< 30 seconds) and tracks with no silence gracefully
- Ensure HLS transcode streams work correctly with the Web Audio pipeline
- Add telemetry/logging for Smart Speed engagement (optional)
- Write documentation for users explaining the feature

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Feature works on Safari, Chrome, Firefox, and Edge
- [ ] No audio glitches on track transitions
- [ ] CPU usage is acceptable on a 3-year-old mid-range Android phone
- [ ] HLS transcode playback is unaffected
- [ ] All existing playback tests pass

title: Smart Speed Phase 4: UI integration and state persistence
description:
Add user-facing controls for Smart Speed and ensure state is persisted correctly across sessions.

### Scope
- Add Smart Speed toggle to PlaybackSpeedControl or PlayerSettingsModal
- Persist per-user  setting to the backend
- Handle CastPlayer: Chromecast should receive normal audio (Smart Speed is local-only)
- Update shared/open session playback time reporting to account for compressed time
- Ensure chapter jumps and bookmarks work correctly with Smart Speed active

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Smart Speed toggle is visible and functional in the player UI
- [ ] Setting persists across browser sessions
- [ ] Cast playback ignores Smart Speed (sends normal audio)
- [ ] Chapter jumps land at correct wall-clock positions
- [ ] Bookmarks show correct timestamps regardless of Smart Speed state

title: Smart Speed Phase 3: Silence compression / time-stretching
description:
Compress detected silent segments to shorten overall playback time without pitch distortion.

### Scope
- In the AudioWorklet, when a silence segment is detected, apply time-stretching to compress it by a configurable ratio (default 2.5x)
- Use a lightweight resampling approach suitable for near-silent audio (no need for pitch preservation since it's silence)
- Add configurable compression ratio (e.g., 1.5x - 5x)
- Apply short crossfade ramps at silence boundaries to prevent clicks/pops
- Maintain wall-clock time accuracy:  from the HTMLAudioElement must still reflect the actual playback position

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Silent segments are audibly shortened during playback
- [ ] No audible clicks at silence boundaries
- [ ] Progress bar and time display remain accurate
- [ ] Compression ratio is configurable
- [ ] CPU usage remains reasonable on mobile devices

title: Smart Speed Phase 2: Real-time silence detection engine
description:
Implement an AudioWorklet that analyzes incoming audio buffers and detects silent segments in real-time.

### Scope
- Create  AudioWorklet processor
- Calculate RMS amplitude per buffer block (e.g., 128-512 samples)
- Detect silence when RMS falls below a configurable threshold (default ~-40dBFS)
- Emit silence boundary events (startMs, endMs) to the main thread
- Build a running 'silence map' for the current track that can be queried for seek/jump operations
- Handle track switches and seeks correctly (reset/rebuild silence map)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Silence boundaries are detected accurately for typical audiobook/podcast content
- [ ] Silence map is maintained per track and updated on seek
- [ ] Detection runs without dropping audio frames on desktop and mid-tier mobile
- [ ] Threshold is configurable via a hidden/advanced setting

title: Smart Speed Phase 1: Web Audio API pipeline refactor
description:
Refactor LocalAudioPlayer to support Web Audio API as the foundation for Smart Speed.

### Scope
- Wrap the HTML5 <audio> element in a MediaElementAudioSourceNode inside an AudioContext
- Route audio through an AudioWorklet (or ScriptProcessorNode fallback) even when Smart Speed is OFF, so the pipeline is ready
- Ensure playbackRate changes are applied correctly through the AudioContext path
- Maintain exact parity with current behavior when Smart Speed is disabled
- Add user setting  (default false) to the user settings API and store

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audio plays normally through Web Audio API pipeline with Smart Speed OFF
- [ ] Playback rate changes work correctly
- [ ] No audible degradation or latency introduced
- [ ] Falls back to direct HTML5 Audio if Web Audio API is unavailable
- [ ]  setting is persisted per-user

title: Smart Speed: silence-shortening playback feature
description:
Implement a 'Smart Speed' feature similar to Overcast that dynamically shortens silent gaps in audio playback, allowing users to listen faster without pitch distortion.

## Background
Currently ABS supports fixed playbackRate via HTML5 Audio. Smart Speed would analyze audio in real-time via Web Audio API, detect silences above a threshold, and compress them by a configurable ratio.

## Incremental Plan

### Phase 1: Audio Pipeline Refactor (foundation)
- Refactor LocalAudioPlayer to use Web Audio API (AudioContext + MediaElementAudioSourceNode) instead of direct HTML5 Audio playbackRate
- Maintain backward compatibility: if smart speed is OFF, behavior is identical to today
- Add user setting: enableSmartSpeed (default false)

### Phase 2: Silence Detection Engine
- Implement an AudioWorklet (or ScriptProcessorNode fallback) that analyzes incoming audio buffers
- Detect silence using RMS amplitude threshold (configurable, default ~-40dB)
- Track silence boundaries (start/end timestamps in audio time)

### Phase 3: Time-Stretching / Silence Compression
- When silence is detected, apply time-stretching to compress the silent segment by a ratio (e.g., 2x-4x)
- Use Waveform-resampling or a lightweight time-stretch algorithm (no pitch shift needed for silence)
- Smooth transitions at silence boundaries to avoid clicks

### Phase 4: Integration & Controls
- Add UI toggle for Smart Speed in PlayerSettingsModal
- Add per-library-item smart-speed state persistence
- Update progress tracking to account for smart-speed-compressed time vs wall-clock time
- Ensure CastPlayer and shared sessions handle smart speed gracefully (cast gets normal speed, local gets smart speed)

### Phase 5: Polish & Edge Cases
- Configurable silence threshold and compression ratio
- Performance testing on mobile devices
- Handle chapter jumps / seeks correctly (recalculate silence map)
- Graceful degradation if AudioWorklet is unsupported

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Smart Speed can be enabled/disabled from player settings
- [ ] When enabled, silent portions of audio are audibly shortened during playback
- [ ] Progress bar and time display remain accurate in real (wall-clock) terms
- [ ] No pitch distortion on spoken content
- [ ] Falls back gracefully on browsers without AudioWorklet support
- [ ] Existing playbackRate control continues to work independently

title: Add subfolder support for OpenID logout redirect URI
description: Auth.js has a TODO: 'ABS does currently not support subfolders for installation. If we want to support it we need to include a config for the serverurl'. When using OpenID authentication with a subfolder installation, the post_logout_redirect_uri is constructed incorrectly because it doesn't account for the server base URL config. Acceptance criteria: OpenID logout redirect works correctly when ABS is installed under a subfolder path.

title: Update getOldMediaProgress to use new model
description:
User.js has a TODO: 'Update to new model' on the getOldMediaProgress method. This method currently uses old model patterns and looks up mediaProgress by extraData.libraryItemId. It should be updated to use the current model structure properly.

## Scope
- Make getOldMediaProgress(libraryItemId, episodeId) async
- When episodeId is provided, look up mediaProgress by mediaItemId === episodeId directly
- When episodeId is null, look up the libraryItem by libraryItemId to get its mediaId, then find mediaProgress by mediaItemId === mediaId
- For podcast library items without episodeId, return null (they require an episodeId)
- Remove the TODO comment
- Update LibraryItemController.js and MeController.js callers to use await (already done in previous attempt)
- DO NOT touch findBookmark or any other methods

## Acceptance Criteria
- getOldMediaProgress works correctly with the new model
- Old TODO is removed
- findBookmark method is preserved
- Existing tests pass
notes: Agent status: Task aborted before any work was done. Worktree still contains uncommitted changes from a previous agent session. CRITICAL BUG: server/models/User.js has findBookmark accidentally replaced with a duplicate getOldMediaProgress. Need to restore findBookmark and properly commit the getOldMediaProgress async refactor. Controllers LibraryItemController.js and MeController.js already use await.
